title: WSJ: Putin's $26B longevity program targets gene-therapy aging slowdown by 2030
description: WSJ reports Russia's government has announced a $26 billion state-funded longevity initiative — championed personally by Putin — with goals including a gene-therapy treatment to slow cellular aging plus bioprinted human tissue and mini-pig organ transplants by 2030. Marks the field's first major state-actor capital deployment at this scale, reframing longevity as geopolitical strategy alongside the US private-capital and Hevolution-led Saudi public investment.

title: BioWorld ARDD 2025 recap: in vivo rejuvenation pivots from reset to 'rewind'
description: BioWorld's deep-dive on ARDD 2025 documents the field's pivot away from full Yamanaka 'reset' toward partial-reprogramming 'rewind' strategies — direct context for Life Biosciences ER-100 glaucoma Phase 1 and Daewoong's Turn Bio acquisition. Reinforces partial reprogramming as the dominant in-vivo rejuvenation paradigm heading into 2026 trial readouts.

title: Cure.com VC map: $67B 2035 longevity market with seven major active funders
description: Comprehensive VC map identifies Khosla Ventures, Apollo Health Ventures, Longevity Vision Holding, LongeVC, Obvious Ventures, age1, and Andreessen Horowitz a16z Bio as the seven most active longevity funders heading into 2H 2026 — total deployable capital exceeds $120B across funds. Underscores the field's structural shift to investable category with a projected $67.03B market by 2035.

title: ABT-263 topical senolytic confirmed to improve wound healing in aged skin — clinical implications scale
description: ScienceDaily aging coverage and viral social distribution confirms the ABT-263 (Navitoclax) topical Bcl-2/Bcl-xL inhibitor dramatically improves wound healing in older skin by clearing senescent fibroblasts — extending the precision-senolytic indication map into dermatology alongside Rubedo's RLS-1496 Phase 1 in plaque psoriasis and atopic dermatitis.

title: Fight Aging: Astrocyte intervention pipeline matures with HDAC inhibitor + Hevin gene therapy
description: Fight Aging's astrocyte-aging review consolidates two converging interventions: an HDAC inhibitor that reduces astrocyte inflammatory overactivation and a gene therapy that increases astrocytic Hevin to improve synaptic function and cognitive performance. Together with the PM20D1/N-oleoyl-leucine microglial amyloid clearance finding and the GZMK CD8+ T cell axis, dementia geroscience now spans three coordinated cell-type interventions.

title: Putin's $26 Billion Longevity Push
description: WSJ reports Russian state scientists are developing gene-therapy treatments to slow cellular aging as centerpiece of $26 billion anti-aging initiative personally championed by Vladimir Putin; program goals include bioprinted human tissue and mini-pig organ transplants by 2030.

title: ARDD 2025: hitting rewind, not reset, for in vivo rejuvenation
description: BioWorld ARDD 2025 conference recap details the field's shift from full Yamanaka reset to partial-reprogramming 'rewind' approaches, consistent with Life Biosciences ER-100 glaucoma Phase 1 and Daewoong-Turn Bio platform acquisition.

title: 7 VCs Funding Longevity Startups in 2026 — Cure.com map
description: Cure.com profiles Khosla Ventures, Apollo Health Ventures, Longevity Vision Holding, LongeVC ($35M Fund I + $120M Fund II), Obvious Ventures, age1, and Andreessen Horowitz a16z Bio as the seven most active longevity VCs; market projected to reach $67.03B by 2035.

title: ABT-263 topical senolytic improves wound healing in older skin
description: Topical ABT-263 (Navitoclax, Bcl-2/Bcl-xL inhibitor) dramatically improves wound healing in older skin by clearing senescent fibroblasts — viral distribution this week reinforces dermatology as a near-term precision-senolytic indication.

title: Fight Aging: astrocyte aging interventions — HDAC inhibitor + Hevin gene therapy
description: Two complementary approaches to astrocyte aging are reviewed: an HDAC inhibitor reducing inflammatory overactivation and a gene therapy increasing astrocytic Hevin to improve synaptic function and cognitive performance in animal studies.

title: Fight Aging: APOE2 promotes DNA signaling pathways resisting cellular senescence
description: Cell-culture and mouse data show APOE2 is associated with stronger DNA-repair signaling and resistance to senescence-like phenotypes in neurons, while APOE4 shows opposite pattern — mechanistic basis for APOE2's longevity-protective effect.

title: Fight Aging: Hallmarks of Aging now field's dominant organizing framework
description: Philosophy-of-science perspective argues the Hallmarks of Aging has become geroscience's dominant organizing framework — structuring collaboration, funding, and research priorities even amid ongoing theoretical disagreement on aging's root cause.

title: Elysium Health launches TELI physician-led longevity institute
description: Elysium Health launched The Elysium Longevity Institute (TELI), a physician-led, science-first comprehensive longevity program led by new CMO Omar Baker MD with Lenny Guarente as chief scientist; initial enrollment capped at 10,000; Q3 2026 launch.

title: Bar-Ilan / NIA / Tel Aviv SIRT6 chromatin age-reversal in old mouse livers
description: Cohen lab plus NIA and Tel Aviv University published in Nature Communications that SIRT6 overexpression in old mice reversed age-related chromatin accessibility changes and inflammation in liver — DNA organization restored toward younger state.

title: Rapamycin News next-gen clock meta-analysis: semaglutide -9% DunedinPACE
description: Meta-analysis of interventions tested in next-gen human clocks: FTC/TAF -6.33 years PhenoAge, omega-3 -6.1 years DamAge, semaglutide -4.9 years PhenoAge / -9% DunedinPACE. Nicotinamide riboside, rapamycin, and D+Q/fisetin senolytics show no significant signal — major credibility update.

title: AGE 2026 Session 4: Junevity siRNA reprogramming, BioAge Morgen, Rubedo GPX4 senolytic Phase 1
description: AGE 2026 Session 4 surfaces translational pipeline: A4LI longevity policy, Junevity siRNA cell reprogramming, BioAge Morgen, and Rubedo Life Sciences' GPX4-modulating selective senolytic in Phase 1 for inflammatory skin / aging skin — GPX4 confirmed as new precision-senolytic axis.

title: Aged CD8+ T cells / GZMK secretion drives hippocampal cognitive decline
description: Aged circulating CD8+ T cells and their secreted factor GZMK drive hippocampal aging signatures and cognitive decline — ameliorated by targeting these cells or GZMK — formalizing a druggable immune-brain-aging node.

title: Daewoong acquires Turn Bio partial-reprogramming platform
description: Korean pharma Daewoong acquired Turn Biotechnologies' partial-epigenetic-reprogramming platform and assets, marking Asia's first major M&A move into OSK reprogramming as a longevity modality.

title: Scribe Therapeutics tests epigenetic treatment to lower cholesterol without altering DNA
description: Scribe Therapeutics is testing an epigenetic editing approach to durably lower cholesterol without altering the DNA sequence, joining Eli Lilly's gene-editing cholesterol program in the converging cardiovascular-aging therapeutics space.

title: Alzinova advances ALZ-101 vaccine to Phase 2
description: Alzinova advanced ALZ-101 Alzheimer's vaccine to Phase 2 trial following positive Phase 1 safety results, adding another active-immunization approach to the neurodegeneration aging pipeline.

title: Insilico + Human Longevity AI longevity foundation model collaboration
description: Insilico Medicine and Human Life Foundation Models (HLI subsidiary) signed multi-million-dollar collaboration to build industry-first AI foundation models for longevity science, combining Pharma.AI with HLI multi-omic clinical data.

title: AstraZeneca tozorakimab Phase III MIRANDA COPD primary endpoint hit
description: AstraZeneca's anti-IL-33 antibody tozorakimab met its primary endpoint in Phase III MIRANDA COPD trial, validating IL-33/ST2 as a druggable inflammaging axis in a major geriatric respiratory disease.

title: Sky Labs CART BP pro included in Korean hypertension guidelines
description: Sky Labs' CART BP pro cuff-less blood pressure monitor included in Korean hypertension guidelines — notable regulatory acceptance of continuous cuff-less BP monitoring as a clinical aging-cardiovascular tool.

title: MBX Biosciences peptide pipeline update for metabolic disorders
description: MBX Biosciences set to share updates on peptide therapies for metabolic disorders — extending the GLP-1-class era of metabolic geroprotection following semaglutide's strong epigenetic clock signal.

title: Multiple biotechs race for sarcopenia (muscle-wasting) regulatory milestones
description: Longevity.Technology flags competitive race among biotechs toward regulatory milestones for muscle-wasting therapies — sarcopenia emerging as next major aging-indication category behind cognitive decline and atherosclerosis.

title: InsightAce: longevity & anti-senescence therapy market growth forecast 2030
description: InsightAce released its updated longevity and anti-senescence therapy market assessment with revenue forecasts through 2030 across senolytic, gene, cell, mitochondrial, and immunotherapy modalities for indications spanning cardiovascular, neurodegenerative, ophthalmology, and cancer.

title: Outside Magazine: longevity and biohacking industry valued at $30B
description: Outside Magazine catalogs the major players in the now-$30B longevity and biohacking consumer industry — reinforcing structural shift from movement to industry alongside Vitalist Bay, Elysium TELI, and Florida clinic expansion.

title: Tau-targeting drug slows early Alzheimer's progression
description: New tau-targeting therapy reported clinically meaningful slowing of early Alzheimer's progression, extending the neurodegeneration pipeline beyond amyloid into a second major druggable mechanism.

title: Repair Biotechnologies REP-0004 mRNA-LNP atherosclerosis-reversal candidate detailed
description: Repair Biotechnologies REP-0004 — liver-targeted mRNA lipid-nanoparticle therapy designed to reduce intracellular free cholesterol, regress atherosclerotic plaque, and reverse fatty liver disease — second damage-clearance modality alongside Cyclarity's UDP-003.

title: Tyshkovskiy/Gladyshev publish universal mammalian aging clocks
description: Universal biological aging markers shared across mammalian species provide a framework for screening interventions in mice and rapidly translating to humans — candidate-identification engine for lifespan-extending therapies.
